_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**COST ALERT — Monitor carrier rate structures for Phase 3 equipment cost pass-through beginning now.**
The first MY 2027-compliant vehicles enter commercial service in calendar year 2026 (manufacturers begin MY 2027 production ahead of the model year). Carriers taking delivery of new compliant equipment will face higher acquisition costs. The workspace should begin tracking whether preferred US trucking subcontractors are communicating fuel surcharge adjustments, equipment premiums, or rate renegotiation requests tied to fleet renewal. *No publicly available per-route cost figure exists; this is a monitoring and early-warning action, not a response to a published rate change.* Action: assign operations lead to contact top-three US road carriers and request fleet renewal timeline and any anticipated rate impact communication by Q3 2026.

**MONITORING — Assess Scope 3 road freight emissions data readiness.**
If the workspace is subject to or preparing for Scope 3 GHG disclosure (under any applicable framework — CSRD for EU-nexus operations, SEC climate disclosure rules if applicable, or voluntary CDP/SBTi reporting), the GHG intensity of US trucking subcontractors is a Category 4 (upstream transportation) or Category 9 (downstream transportation) input depending on transaction structure. Phase 3 fleet transition will affect these figures. Action: confirm whether preferred US carriers can supply activity-based or spend-based emissions data for FY 2026 reporting cycles. Flag as a data gap if not.

**MONITORING — Track legal status of the Phase 3 rule under current administration.**
The EPA source page (last updated February 13, 2026) references a related item: "Final Rule: Rescission of the Greenhouse Gas Endangerment Finding and Motor Vehicle Greenhouse Gas Emission Standards Under the Clean Air Act." *This document notes that item as existing on the EPA source page; the workspace should confirm with legal counsel whether any rescission action affects the Phase 3 rule's current enforceability.* **Legal Confirmation Required.** Action: brief legal counsel on the rescission reference and obtain a written status opinion on Phase 3 enforceability before briefing clients on US road freight emissions compliance.

*Source: EPA, "Regulations for Greenhouse Gas Emissions from Commercial Trucks & Buses," last updated February 13, 2026. https://www.epa.gov/regulations-emissions-vehicles-and-engines/regulations-greenhouse-gas-emissions-commercial-trucks*
*Source: Federal Register 89 FR 29440, April 22, 2024.*
